---
name: Dogfood feedback
about: Share what happened when you tried the workspace on your own AI collaboration. Experience only, never private material.
title: "[Dogfood]: "
labels: feedback
assignees: ""
---

> Report your **experience**, never your **material**. Every field asks how it felt or what you noticed — not the task you ran or the work you reviewed. Do **not** paste real task content, customer or contact material, tokens or credentials, absolute local paths, private chat logs, or company-internal information. Illustrate with a synthetic placeholder or words instead. We do not collect your private originals — the open method runs on your own machine. Full list: [What NOT to upload](../../docs/DOGFOOD.md#what-not-to-upload).

## Which AI tools do you use

Names of tools only (Claude Code / Codex / Cursor / Copilot / Cline / Windsurf / a chat AI / other).

## Your original pain point

Context loss / AI claiming false completion / re-explaining background across sessions / not knowing which AI is right / other. Describe the **pain**, not the task it happened on.

## Where the first experience stalled

Which step of the 10-minute or 30-minute path, and what was unclear or broke. Describe the **step**, not the content you fed in.

## Most useful mechanism

Which mechanism helped most (e.g. dual-guard / handoff / acceptance / harvest), and one line on why.

## Mechanism you did not understand

Which one read as jargon or where you got lost. This is the most useful thing you can tell us.

## Would you use this on a real workflow

Yes / no / maybe, and the one thing that would tip it. Your verdict and the blocker, not a description of the real workflow itself.

## Would paying to save calibration time be worth it

Yes / no / not sure. Just gauging interest — no numbers, no commitment.

## Anything else

One thing that surprised you, one thing that annoyed you.

## Privacy check

- [ ] Every answer above is about my experience, not my actual task content.
- [ ] I included no customer or contact material, tokens, credentials, absolute local paths, private chat logs, or company-internal information.
